emergent_themes.description: Strong preference for familiar choices in both cooking and ordering to avoid disappointment
Awareness that subscriptions increase spending while claiming to save money
Preference for integrated services (Uber ecosystem) over single-purpose apps
Viewing food primarily as necessity rather than pleasure, despite enjoying eating
Structured use of delivery as emotional reward system tied to weekly routine

coding_summary: P003 demonstrates a highly systematic and risk-averse approach to food and delivery. Key patterns include: (1) Strong Friday reward ritual using delivery as structured emotional regulation, (2) Risk aversion in both cooking and ordering - sticks to familiar choices to avoid disappointment, (3) Functional relationship with food - views cooking as necessary but not enjoyable despite significant time investment in batch cooking, (4) Clear preference for Uber ecosystem over single-purpose apps due to integrated benefits, (5) Awareness of subscription spending paradox - knows Uber One increases spending while claiming savings, (6) Price consciousness balanced with convenience needs, (7) Strong desire for group payment splitting features, (8) Safety concerns about rider work as young woman. Her behavior reveals tension between stated preferences (dislikes cooking) and actual behavior (always cooks weeknights, batch cooks regularly).
coverage_gaps: No coverage gaps - all research objectives received primary codes from this participant's rich and detailed responses across all topic areas.
